rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 8, 2023, line 4. Defendant's Demurrer to the Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend and without leave to amend. The demurrer to the first cause of action for Creditor's Suit is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege which defendants have possession or control of which properties; when the properties were purchased and who purchased them; and who they were transferred to and when. The demurrer to the second cause of action for Quiet Title is moot. The demurrer to the third cause of action for Fraudulent Transfer is overruled. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action Conspiracy to Defraud is sustained with leave to amend. The allegations in 34 relating to the conspiracy to transfer 547 23rd Avenue references the Yans and defendants Fang and Tu only. Plaintiff to allege specific facts tying Defendant T24 LLC to a conspiracy with respect to the property. Plaintiff must allege when he discovered the facts constituting the fraudulent transfer. (CC 338(d).) The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for Theft is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot set forth facts establishing the first element of the claim: that the property has been stolen or has been obtained in any manner constituting theft. (Penal Code 496, subd. (a).) The element requires that when the property in question came into the defendant's hands, it already had the character of having been stolen. Plaintiff's status as a judgment creditor does not turn him into the owner of Mr. Yan's properties such that he can maintain a cause of action for theft of his property. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. SHUZHEN TU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 8, 2023, line 4. Defendant's Motion to Expunge Lis pendens is DENIED. Plaintiff has alleged a real property claim. (Kirkeby v. Superior Court (2004) 33 Cal.4th 642, 649.) Plaintiff has established by the preponderance of the evidence a probable validity to the claim for fraudulent transfer. rulings.Case Title: XELAN PROP 1, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. RECEIVERSHIP SPECIALISTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT RECEIVERSHIP SPECIALISTS, INC., KEVIN (RECEIVER) SINGER, ERVIN COHEN & JESSUP, LLP, BLAKE ALSBROOK DEFENDANTS NOTICE OF MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 8, 2023, line 13. Defendant's Special Motion to Strike (Anti-SLAPP) is is GRANTED. On April 19, 2023, the Court continued this motion to May 5, 2023, granting Defendant an opportunity to cure the previously rejected opposition filings. Defendant was instructed to file the opposition by April 21, 2023. On April 27, 2023, six days after the deadline provided by the Court, Defendant filed the requested opposition papers, which were once again rejected. The only filing before the Court is the Kihagi Declaration, filed on April 7, 2023. Defendant's declaration, without a memorandum, was considered. Plaintiff showed that the causes of actions against Defendant Receiver arose from protected speech or petitioning activities of the Receivership. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence that Defendant Receiver was not protected by quasi-judicial immunity; Plaintiff did not establish a probability of prevailing on any causes of action (Code Civ. Proc., 425.16.) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)
